intro.title: One Small Step
intro.body: Apollo 11 lifted off from Cape Kennedy on 16 July 1969 with Armstrong, Aldrin, and Collins aboard. Eagle landed in the Sea of Tranquility on 20 July at 20:17 UTC; Armstrong stepped onto the surface at 02:56 UTC on 21 July. They splashed down in the Pacific four days later.

stops.name: Cape Kennedy (Pad 39A)
stops.note: Lift-off 13:32 UTC, 16 July 1969. Saturn V SA-506 throws 2,800 tonnes off the pad.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate, Kodachrome saturation. Saturn V at dawn lift-off, flame trench glowing orange, condensation cone forming around the nose cap.

stops.name: Trans-Lunar Injection
stops.note: S-IVB third-stage burn at 2 h 44 min after launch — symbolic mid-Atlantic position. The CSM/LM stack heads for the Moon.
stops.imagePrompt: Kodachrome plate. Earth disc from low orbit through a porthole, S-IVB tank visible at the bottom of the frame.

stops.name: Sea of Tranquility
stops.note: Eagle touches down 20:17 UTC, 20 July 1969. Armstrong: 'Houston, Tranquility Base here. The Eagle has landed.'
stops.imagePrompt: Kodachrome plate. Lunar plain in low sun, LM Eagle with shadow stretching long, footprints leading away to a planted flag.

stops.name: USS Hornet (splashdown)
stops.note: Splashdown 16:50 UTC, 24 July 1969 in the central Pacific. Helicopters pick the crew off the bobbing Command Module.
stops.imagePrompt: Kodachrome plate. Pacific swell, conical Command Module beside an inflatable raft, helicopter overhead, US Navy frogmen in the water.

stops.name: Houston (Mission Control)
stops.note: Crew quarantined three weeks in the Lunar Receiving Laboratory at JSC, then released for the world tour.
stops.imagePrompt: Kodachrome plate. Glass-walled quarantine trailer, three crew in flight suits waving from inside, Mission Control sign behind.
